title: Star Wars Kids (1997)
content:
{{Top|rwm|italics=1}}
[[File:Star_Wars_Kids_Lando_web.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The cover of the [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] issue (#9)]]
'''''Star Wars Kids''''' was a magazine published by [[Scholastic]] from [[July]] [[1997]] to [[February]] [[1999]]. It was home delivered as a supplement to the subscription to ''[[Star Wars Missions]]'', with a new issue each month. There were twenty issues in total, with each one highlighting a certain character from the [[Original trilogy]] and a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] from either the films or the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]].

==Regular features==
{{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Star Wars Kids}}

===Brain Blasters===
A quiz, comprising approximately fifteen questions, all to do with the Original trilogy and the Expanded Universe.

===''Star Wars Kids'' Talks To…===
An interview with an actor portraying the selected character of the month, be it [[Mark Hamill]] for the [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] issue, [[Harrison Ford]] for the [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] issue, or [[David Prowse]], [[James Earl Jones]] and [[Sebastian Shaw]] for the [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] issue.

===Space Science===
This section contained educational information about real-world space and compared it to a location or item in the fictional ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe.

===Who Is That Alien?===
Two fact files about a certain [[Alien/Legends|alien]] species, seen either in the films or the EU.

===On Our Planet===
A section comparing a climate on Earth with one of the planets from the ''Star Wars'' universe.

===Yoda's Puzzle Pages===
This section consisted of crosswords and wordsearches that were related to the ''Star Wars'' universe.

===''Star Wars Missions'' Bonus Page===
This page would be a supplement to the [[roleplaying game]] found in the ''Star Wars Missions'' scenario. To play this properly, the user would require the equipment delivered with the ''Star Wars Missions'' starter pack.

===A-Maze-Ing Universe===
A maze for readers to play though that would usually relate to the selected planet for the month.

===Comic===
Each issue would contain one-fifth of a complete short comic story. There were four comics in total: ''[[The Rebel Thief]]'', ''[[X-Wing Marks the Spot]]'', ''[[Imperial Spy]]'', and "[[Death Star Pirates]]." There was also a short comic called ''[[The Gambler's Quest]]'' in Issue #12.
